title: Generics, Constraints, Inference, and Variance · Independent Type Trial
summary: Build reusable selectors and repositories that preserve relationships between keys, inputs, outputs, and callbacks. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a relationship-preserving generic data toolkit with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Introduce a type parameter only when multiple positions share a relationship that callers need preserved.
Constrain operations while returning the caller-specific subtype rather than widening to the constraint.
Use keyof and indexed access types to connect property names with their corresponding value types.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
